content.full_text:
05/08/92 14:18 ☎ 805 546 3642 PAK MAIL SLO P.01

May 7, 1992
1075 Capistrano Ct.
San Luis Obispo
Ca. 93403
805-541-2615


John Wallace
FPPC, Legal Division
PO Box 807
Sacramento, Ca. 95804-0807
916-327-2026 FPPC FAX


Dear John,

I appreciated your call back regarding San Luis Obispo County campaign activities I will be involved in. Here is the samples of what my yet-unnamed group of local citizens would like to put out.

Enclosed is a "Fact Sheet" on the upcoming measure to raise property taxes to pay for a new county General Hospital. We want to put it out as is. Is there any changes or additions that should be made? We consider all the information to be facts and even though we don't say so on the sheet, feel it will support those opposing this upcoming ballot measure.

We would also like to raise money to hire a consultant to help coordinate the campaign. And we would like to hire a private company to do surveys to determine voter opinions regarding the overall issue. Both would cost over $1000. If we do, we need to declare anything since the surveys and consultants would be helping us educate the voters about this measure?

We will clearly be working to defeat this measure, but will never actually come out and say "Vote against the Hospital Tax Measure", but "Vote on the Hospital Tax Measure".

Can we do any of these without having to declare ourselves as a PAC? This is important because I have this group and one other unnamed group that wants to work on this right away for both this measure and the county supervisorial primary campaigns. We do need an answer within 21 days, or sooner, if possible.

Thank you for your assistance.

Sincerely,

Ron Bearce
Ron Bearce

05/08/92 14:17 # 805 546 3642 PRK MAIL SLO P.02

General Hospital Measure Fact Sheet

The County of San Luis Obispo has placed a measure on the local election ballot.
The measure is a major tax increase that will affect all property owners for decades
to come, and will result in negative economic impacts on residents and businesses.

COUNTY COSTS ARE INCREASING. The costs of the county budget continue to increase at a rate
faster than the population increase.

THE QUALITY OF HEALTH CARE IS IMPORTANT TO EVERYONE IN SAN LUIS OBISPO
COUNTY. This measure is allow the county to raise taxes on everyone within the county. But the improvements will not necessarily provide a better health care environment for both the patients and public
at the hospital site.

WILL NONE OF THE BOND MONIES BE USED FOR COUNTY ADMINISTRATOR SALARIES?
Surveys have shown public opposition to raising taxes to pay for administrator salaries. This money
could go for such purposes.

THE BOND MONIES FROM THE TAX INCREASE WILL BE USED TO make renovations to build
a new hospital when more affordable alternatives are available.

OTHER PROGRAMS WILL SUFFER. Due to insufficient funds, the county has to cut other programs
and services during the last few years. If the tax does not pass, money could be used in the private sector
while implementing more affordable alternatives.

THIS IS LIKE A LOTTERY FOR NEW BUILDINGS in that the government is gambling that a new
tax increase will win them millions now while destabilizing our local economy later.
